title: ⚔️ Chapter boss ⭐⭐⭐: my home and my family's things

questions.prompt: Complete: "In the living room there ___ a sofa, two armchairs and a carpet."
questions.explanation: In a list, the verb follows the FIRST noun. Here the list starts with "a sofa" (singular), so we use "there is": there is a sofa, two armchairs and a carpet. The common trap is looking at "two armchairs" and choosing "are" — but the first item decides.

questions.prompt: Complete: "Sami and Nour are my friends. I visit ___ every Friday, and ___ visit me too."
questions.explanation: After the verb "visit", the object pronoun is "them" (I visit them). Before the verb "visit", the subject pronoun is "they" (they visit me). The common trap is mixing them up: the doer is a subject (they), the receiver is an object (them).

questions.prompt: "The bedroom of my two sisters" — choose the correct genitive form: "my ___ bedroom."
questions.explanation: There are two sisters (a plural owner ending in -s), so the apostrophe goes AFTER the -s: my sisters' bedroom ✓. "Sister's" means only one sister, and "sisters / sisters's" are wrong forms for a plural owner.

questions.prompt: Complete: "Yesterday I bought ___ shirt. ___ shirt is dark blue."
questions.explanation: The first time you mention something new, use "a": I bought a shirt. The second time, you both know which shirt, so use "the": The shirt is dark blue ✓. The common trap is starting with "the" for something the listener does not know yet.

questions.prompt: On a shelf, the clock is on the left and the vase is on the right. Where is the photo, if it sits exactly between them?
questions.explanation: The photo is in the centre of the shelf, so we say it is "in the middle" ✓. "Between" would need to name two things (between the clock and the vase); on its own it is incomplete, and "near / next to" only say it is close to something.

questions.prompt: Complete: "There ___ five ___ playing in the yard."
questions.explanation: "Five children" is plural, so we need "there are" ✓, and the plural of child is the irregular "children". The traps: "there is" does not agree with a plural, while "childs" and "childrens" are impossible forms.
